<edit>
<<<<<<< SEARCH
## DC Wiring and Combiner Boxes
Run 10 AWG PV wire in continuous conduit from the array to Combiner Box A, located on the north parapet wall. Keep DC and AC raceways separated by at least 1 inch, per NEC Article 690.31. Each combiner accepts four strings fused at 15 A; do not mix 430 W and older 370 W modules on the same fuse.

Grounding electrode conductors must be bare copper, sized per Table 250.66, and bonded to the rail system with listed lay-in lugs. Double-check that rapid shutdown devices are within 1 foot of the array boundary, as required by the 2023 NEC update adopted in this jurisdiction on January 1, 2024.

=======
>>>>>>> REPLACE

## System Architecture
The backbone runs on redundant 10 Gbps fiber links between nodes. Each site hosts identical reference monitors—Genelec 8351B—calibrated to 83 dB SPL at the mix position. We use a custom Dante/AES67 bridge developed by engineer Lin Zhou, deployed first on March 14, 2024.

<edit>
<<<<<<< SEARCH
## Major Platform Releases
On August 22, 2024, we shipped SentinelGrid Core 4.7.0 across all global nodes. The release introduces the Cortex AI detection engine, which processes over 12 billion events per day with sub-200ms latency. Key milestones include:
- Full integration of the ZeroTrust Access Gateway (ZTAG-9000) for hybrid cloud workloads
- Native support for MITRE ATT&CK v14.1 mapping in incident timelines
- Enhanced SOAR playbooks for automated containment of supply-chain intrusions

## Client Portal & Service Updates

The Meridian Client Gateway received a major update on May 15, 2024. Users now have access to predictive ETAs powered by machine-learning forecasts trained on 2.3 million historical delivery records.

Service-level commitments have been revised as follows:
- Standard ground: delivery within 3-5 business days
- Priority ground: delivery within 2 business days
- White-glove installation: 48-hour scheduling with dedicated crew assignment

For technical support or contract inquiries, contact the Logistics Support Center at 1-800-555-0199 or via the updated portal messaging system.
